title: Possible inconsistencies with Rei and Gendo
display_title: Possible inconsistencies with Rei and Gendo
wikitext:
[]: Possible inconsistencies with Rei and Gendo             Quote Post by xPearse » Fri Jul 18, 2014 7:18 pm Please help clear this up for me, me and my bro were talking again and brought up several points that kinda don't make sense as they sorta clash with others. As from the title its about Rei and Gendo, here are the several points. Rei can be seen as sorta not caring about herself, she doesn't clean the blood off her sheets or clean up after herself. Trash on the floor and dirty dishes in the sink, if this is the case then why does she bother to clean herself by showering. It would kinda make sense if she didn't, slept in her school clothes, never washed her hair and so forth but she always seems so clean in the anime. I guess smelly people arent hoot and that would put people off but it would make sense seeing how can be seen as having a lack of self worth so again why clean this but not that. Me and my bro just thought about it and said it doesn't make any sense taking into account various other facts. There was this and Rei not locking her door we discussed for her character, I can't remember which episode it was but Rei is reading a book at school and Asuka appears. Rei looks out of the corner of her eye annoyed by Asuka's presence showing she does care about her own privacy or at least in a sense so again, taking this into account why doesn't she lock her door. We think this also doesn't make any sense, now onto Gendo. He cares for Rei and needs her for his plan at the same time being his surrogate daughter so why would he let anyone intefer with her development such as Shinji if she could possibly stray from him. It doesn't make any sense, also taking into account he hates Shinji (What me and my bro think, with good reason) so why would he let a bad influence like him affect Rei. I was also thinking this when I watched the show, it doesn't make any sense. Anyhow enough of what we think, what's your thoughts on this.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Jul 18, 2014 7:43 pm Gendo never thought that Rei would have any reason to stray away from him, hence his surprise in EoE when she stole Adam from him to go to Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jul 18, 2014 8:06 pm Human beings are mishmashes of apparent contradictions that are really just misconceptions on the observers' part as to what the "rule" they see being violated actually is. When something seems like a contradiction, it usually takes only a modicum of extra effort to come up with a number of possibilities as to the actual underlying rule that satisfies both conditions. Rei leaves her personal spaces to rot, yet cleans herself. It could be as simple as that she is motivated to clean herself by a feeling of simple ickiness, like I am, whereas she is not bothered by the mess in her personal space. I shower at least once a day (and already twice today), but the floor of my room is currently littered with black lint that I really need to vacuum but just never get around to because it doesn't bother me enough. Another possibility -- perhaps the more likely one -- is that Gendo ordered her to shower regularly. As to the second point, from what I remember Rei moved because Asuka was blocking her light, not because she wanted privacy. As to the third point, the movie addresses this directly. Have you seen EoE?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jul 18, 2014 8:08 pm I assume she's been instructed to maintain a respectable public appearance but nobody has bothered to tell her she ought to give a shit about her apartment.

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Fri Jul 18, 2014 11:09 pm My personal space is a mess, but I take showers everyday. Misato's apartment is a minefield before Shinji moves in, but she takes showers fairly often. This is a common trait of people and not at all surprising

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jul 18, 2014 11:15 pm Misato’s room is cluttered so that she doesn’t have to be. It’s the equilibrium of the universe that must not be tampered with.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Jul 19, 2014 2:26 am Monk Ed wrote: Another possibility -- perhaps the more likely one -- is that Gendo ordered her to shower regularly.

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Sat Jul 19, 2014 6:25 am I know we can possibly assume it was either Gendo or Ritsuko that told her to but I can't help but feel otherwise about this. It would fit in with her character and they could have had a scene of someone telling her to wash further showing she doesn't care about herself. I know you say she could naturally feel icky and then from that shower but again I'd like to think otherwise, also take into account that Rei isn't exactly a normal person like Misato so the situation is different. As for Gendo, he isn't an idiot. He must know the consequences of anyone's interaction other than himself on Rei and yea I have seen EOE, i didn't like it. Anyway I'm always talking about the original TV series without any of that background information such as CI and all that other stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Jul 19, 2014 6:47 am You missed the point in my previous post : Rei likes water and would have no trouble in bathing even if she was not ordered to. http://wiki.evageeks.org/Theory_and_Analysis:Rei%27s_Monologue "Water. Something comforting. Commander Ikari" - these aren't the words of a pig in human clothing!

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Sat Jul 19, 2014 7:33 am UrsusArctos wrote: You missed the point in my previous post : Rei likes water and would have no trouble in bathing even if she was not ordered to. http://wiki.evageeks.org/Theory_and_Analysis:Rei%27s_Monologue "Water. Something comforting. Commander Ikari" - these aren't the words of a pig in human clothing!

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Jul 20, 2014 6:04 pm It is a common misconception that Gendo keeps a tight lock on her and manipulates her every mood, but that is simply not what the series shows - He's distantly affectionate in a genuine enough way, but generally neglectful. He's got a grasp on statistic and big picture things, organizations as a whole, he manipulates events from afar like billiard balls, but he does NOT have Yagami-esque insight into individual humans, and neglecting/ not having any true insight into Ritsuko or Rei leads to their betrayal. Fuyutsuki is a lesser case, in that Gendo seems to appreciate him, but Fuyutsuki totally hates his guts, although he doesn't have the will to betray him. In fact, he states that the "formless bonds between people" are very opaque to him - with most people, he just doesn't care/ assumes they hate him anyway, but in the case of Shinji, he actually cares, so he's terrified of not knowing. He probably has an inherent lacking of some sort here, although he could have taken different paths. He's basically given up on the world after Yui's death and can't summon up much in terms of emotional energy. Seeing Rei as his "daughter" (and that of Yui, whom he basicaly worships and never questions, more like a god or symbol than a fellow human being ), it just never occurred to him that she might betray him. He didn't consider her PoV - She never complained, she seemed to be selfsufficient, so he saw no reason to worry. I see that line as womb water symbolism as a motherly feeling for children feels comfortable but I guess it's up for interpretation.

As from the title its about Rei and Gendo, here are the several points. Rei can be seen as sorta not caring about herself, she doesn't clean the blood off her sheets or
